text: Story Q&A: ask me anything about the brand / next drops
note: Direct connection; surfaces what people want.

text: Models: feature a local athlete / waterman in the gear
note: Aspirational but real to the community.

text: Throwback Thursday: the Kalohe Kai song that defined the summer
note: Tie the era to a current drop. Island-reggae nostalgia.

text: BTS: the rejected designs — what didn't make the cut and why
note: Transparency builds taste and trust.

text: BTS: picking fabric / approving a sample on camera
note: Show the craft and the standards.

text: Reel: 'POV you pull up to a Westside potluck in Musubi'
note: Relatable local humor. Highly shareable.

text: Story series: a day on the Westside, tagged Musubi
note: Behind-the-life content keeps you top of feed.

text: Product: restock alert with a 'last time it sold out in X' line
note: Scarcity + social proof.

text: Throwback: old-school flyer aesthetic recreated with the new tee
note: Nostalgia + product in one frame.

text: Carousel: the meaning behind the design — slide-by-slide
note: Cultural context earns product posts their place.

text: Story poll: which colorway drops next? Let followers pick
note: High engagement, signals you listen to the community.

text: Throwback Thursday: island-reggae playlist that fits the brand
note: Pair with the JB OS Vibe player.

text: Reel: how the tee is folded + packed (no voiceover, brand soundtrack)
note: Process content builds trust faster than product shots.

text: Throwback: childhood Westside spot revisited in current gear
note: Place-based nostalgia.

text: Ohana: matching parent + keiki fits at the beach
note: Heart-tug content; great for shares.

text: "Auntie's favorite tee" — let a real auntie style it her way
note: Real person, real fit. No model posing.

text: Story: poll two caption options, use the winner on the next post
note: Co-create with the audience.

text: Sunrise spam-musubi run — film the Westside drive + first light
note: Lifestyle reel; pair with the conditions/golden-hour times in JB OS.

text: Story: tide + surf check of the morning (use the JB OS data)
note: Daily ritual content followers return for.

text: Throwback Thursday: first-ever Musubi photo vs now
note: Growth story. People love an origin arc.

text: Reel: 'getting ready' get-ready-with-me in a Musubi tee
note: GRWM format adapted to the brand.

text: Models: pau-hana crew shot, everyone in a different piece
note: Group energy; shows range without flat-lays.

text: Carousel: 3 ways to wear one Musubi tee, Westside edition
note: Beach → town → pau hana. Saveable styling content.

text: Region-real mockup: tee on a Waiʻanae backdrop, not a studio
note: Previews should read local and authentic.

text: Product drop teaser — close-ups, no full reveal, countdown sticker
note: Build anticipation before the drop.

text: Reel: 'things that hit different on the Westside' + tee cameo
note: Trend-style listicle, local flavor.

text: Carousel: care guide — keep your Musubi tee fresh for years
note: Useful = saveable. Subtle quality flex.

text: Reel: unboxing from a real customer (repost UGC)
note: Social proof. Ask permission, credit them.

text: Models call: feature 3 community members + their why
note: UGC-style. Real faces over flat-lays.

text: Local spotlight: shout out a Westside small biz wearing Musubi
note: Community-first. Builds real relationships.

text: "Auntie's favorite tee" — feature a real local auntie wearing it
note: Tie into the auntie's-favorite concept; shoot a genuine local auntie, not a model-y shot.

text: Throwback Thursday — Kalohe Kai / island-reggae era nostalgia
note: Recurring Thursday slot. Lean on the Kalohe Kai reference and that era's vibe.

text: Region-real mockups — tees shot on Westside Oʻahu backdrops
note: Make previews read as authentically local instead of generic studio mockups.

text: Use real community members as models in product shots
note: Shoot actual people from the community for the lifestyle/local feel.

text: Archie in Musubi gear — ohana mini photo shoot
note: Kid-in-the-gear content for the ohana/family angle. Pull from the Archie gear order.

text: Behind the scenes — how a drop comes together (print → fold → pack)
note: Process content builds trust faster than flat product shots. No voiceover, brand soundtrack.
